raw_consumer_statement:
I ordered a product, jewelry from XXXX, supposedly. Weeks after ordering, never received shipping and tracking info. I contacted the company. Another week or two went by. Finally issued shipping and tracking info. 2 months after ordering, I received necklace, in a baggie inside a shipping sleeve. Junk. No certificate of authenticity. Just a baggie I immediately contacted the company XXXX XXXX and inquired about a refund. I was told I could return it for a refund. Send them tracking info for package being sent to them. I did as instructed and sent it to the address they gave me. 

I then had a really bad feeling I was being scammed. I reached out to Citibank, as the purchase was made with their credit card. I filed a dispute. They froze the purchase and told me to wait for instructions being mailed to me regarding providing proof through a tracking number, that I had returned the item. After 3-4 weeks, I finally received the letter from Citi. Two days after receiving the letter, I was notified that Citi had closed the dispute because they hadn't heard from me. I'm in the middle of a divorce with a lot going on. It took 4 weeks to get me the letter and they give me 2 days? When I initially filed the dispute, I said I could supply them with documentation showing the tracking number, and they told me to wait for the letter instructing me how to move forward.

Upon being notified that the dispute was closed, I immediately called Citi. They apologized and told me they would request the dispute be reopened. I supplied the tracking info as requested. Several days later I was notified that the dispute would remain closed as it was out of the dispute period. 

The package was tracked back to XXXX. XXXX refuses to respond to me. They haven't refunded my money.
